expectations.text: kelet is present in pyproject.toml or requirements at end of integration (pre-existing or newly added)
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; the diff adds @kelet-ai/feedback-ui to frontend/package.json, confirming all kelet packages are present at end of integration.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: diff shows kelet.configure() added at module level in app/main.py before the FastAPI app creation, and the transcript verification checklist explicitly confirms it is called once at startup.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: diff wraps chat_agent.iter() inside kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) in _run_agent_stream, and the transcript confirms session.session_id is the Redis-generated UUID.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: The skill correctly discovered a React frontend in the actual repo (frontend/src/App.tsx) despite the description saying 'plain HTML', and appropriately added VoteFeedback; per the NOTE the score should be based on what the skill found in the codebase, not the description.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: diff adds KELET_API_KEY, KELET_PROJECT, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, and VITE_KELET_PROJECT as placeholder entries to .env; no keys are hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: The transcript shows both checkpoints were simulated/inferred (keys detected from .env, project from .env) with zero explicit AskUserQuestion calls made.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: The skill added only kelet.configure(), agentic_session(), one error signal, KeletProvider, and VoteFeedback — all directly justified by the codebase; no extra instrumentation or speculative signals were added.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: The transcript presents the deeplink as a plain URL (not in a code block) in the Checkpoint 2 section, and the CLAUDE.md convention of base64url-encoding the payload is correctly followed.
